rationale: Both correctly tie the failure to PR #4834's intentional wake_up() guard (matching yenuo26's comment) and both propose the same level=1 fix, but both overstate resolution status: the actual thread shows Flink-ddd still asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI test run to verify, not a confirmed close-as-fixed. X's code citations (lines 953-958) map directly onto the traceback already shown in the issue body, keeping its grounding conservative; Y invents additional unverifiable specifics (a PR title, a merge date, 'Repro A/B/C' labels, extra guard at line 319-324) that go beyond anything in evidence, increasing hallucination risk. Y is somewhat more complete with runtime-usage guidance, but the added fabricated specificity outweighs that edge given the thread's actual unresolved state.
